title: About Lilith
wikitext:
[]: About Lilith Post by Thomas68 » Sat Aug 16, 2014 4:30 pm Since the most part of Evangelion is about the Angels and EVAs itself, is Lilith the only demon that appears in Evangelion? Lilith is a female demon and the symbol of evil, A.K.A. the Guardian of Hell Gates and Cerberus (Greek Mythology) along with Hekate, as demon of diseases and death (Mesopotamic Mythology) and as a vampiric legend (Hebrew Mythology). In Evangelion, a lot is mentioned about Lilith. But why? Why Anno didn't used another demon like Mephistopheles or Behemoth? Has Anno had any kind of interest in Lilith? And which Lilith (Greek, Mesopotamic or Hebrew) is used n Evangelion?

[]: Re: About Lilith Post by Firetrout » Sat Aug 16, 2014 4:46 pm Lilith is probably taken from the Abrahamic version of Lilith, otherwise known as the first wife of Adam.

[]: Post by Sorrow » Sat Aug 16, 2014 5:01 pm I don't know any--nor do I have any access to--official information about why such names were used. Though apparently "Neon Genesis Evangelion" was named as such because it "sounds complicated" - or something like that. Given that information I wouldn't feel ridiculous in what I'm about to say: Adam is the first human according to Abrahamic religions and is obviously named as such in reference to that. The first angel. Lilith is the designated name of the second angel as Adam's counterpart. The Abrahamic Lilith was Adam's first wife, was made from the same Earth and at the same time; this would make them equals in many ways. Evangelion, often shortened to Eva, are infamously made from Adam just like Eve was made from Adam's rib. Evangelion -> Eva -> Eve. The rest of the angels appear to be named after other angels from the Abrahamic religions. Them being angels and all; this hardly needs explaining. It seems Anno had no intention of using names of demons and Lilith is the exception due to her relation to Adam. If he had called them "Adam" and "Eve" then he wouldn't have been able to "cleverly" name the Evangelions - given the way in which they were made. Someone else may have official information on why they were named as such but to me it seemed pretty evident and it serves to answer your question as to why other "demon names" weren't used.

[]: Post by Chuckman » Sat Aug 16, 2014 6:28 pm Seek ye here. http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/16014/Beyond-the-Monster-Mother/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15461/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-is-an-Occult-Grimoire/

[]: Post by Sachi » Sat Aug 16, 2014 9:58 pm Lilith's dichotomy with Adam in-series makes it pretty clear that her conceptual origins come from the biblical Lilith. Lilith is Adam's first wife who is his equal, just as the in-series Adam and Lilith are equal due to their nature as Seeds of Life. The biblical Adam wasn't very cool with Lilith being his equal (heaven forbid that the woman gets to ride on top every once in a while, amirite?), so they had a falling out and shortly thereafter Eve is born from Adam, the subservient and submissive gal he'd always wanted. The Evangelion is Eve, born from in-series Adam and is subservient to man. Unit-01 is the special case, born from Lilith, and tends to act out from time to time, eventually even obtaining godlike power of its own; a spiritual successor to the biblical Lilith, at least in terms of individuality and free will. Other somewhat relevant threads: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3275/Religion-in-NGE-Split/ (this one makes me miss Yojimbo) http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11980/Why-does-NGE-have-so-many-references-to-the-Bible/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/402/Religious-Symbols-Just-for-looks/
